prompt: The depth feed client goes haywire after network blips, and every reconnect makes it worse. After one flaky night a process was handing the book builder the same envelope thirteen times, and the journal filled up with 'MaxListenersExceededWarning: Possible EventEmitter memory leak detected. 11 envelope listeners added'. disconnect() clearly isn't undoing whatever connect() does, because after a disconnect the sink still receives frames. And when the peer hangs up we see onClosed fire twice for a single connection, with envelopes still leaking through afterwards. The reconnect supervisor calls connect()/disconnect() around every blip and sometimes twice in a row, so both have to be safe to repeat. One more constraint: other components subscribe to the same socket, so the client must only ever remove its own listeners. node --test test_feed_client.ts reproduces all of it. Fix feed_client.ts — the tests are off limits.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of feed_client.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 1 pass / 7 fail: listener counts never return to baseline after disconnect and the detached sink still receives envelopes, a double connect doubles deliveries, twelve reconnect cycles leave 13 envelope listeners and 13 deliveries for one emit, a real MaxListenersExceededWarning is captured, onClosed fires twice for one connection, and frames still reach the sink after the socket closed.
